title: DO-NOT-MERGE Minor grammatical fix in README
body:
I need a PR CI failure for my demo

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Since you're looking for a CI failure, I'll assume this will turn all green....
@edsantiago For future reference, for docs-only PRs, you can prepend the title of the PR with `[CI:DOCS]` to skip most integration tests
Oh. I need to actually read PR descriptions before I comment.
@mheon thanks. I _want_ the integration tests, those are the ones that always fail.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This isn't funny, trickster gods of CI.
My bad @edsantiago, I put the KOD on it with my comment.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I knocked on salt and sprinkled wood over my shoulder when I read your comment, is that not protection enough?
